title: cylindropuntia thurberi subsp. versicolor
intro: Cylindropuntia thurberi subsp. versicolor, commonly known as a type of cholla cactus, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Cylindropuntia thurberi subsp. versicolor include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu, as well as its potential to soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, salves, or tinctures, which can be used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds.
